title: The Histories
author: Herodotus
content: “If a man insisted always on being serious, and never allowed himself a bit of fun, he would go mad or become unstable without knowing it.”

title: Ajax
author: Sophocles
content: “Not even the strongest man can stand against the will of the gods—or the momentum of his own mistakes.”

title: Sayings of the Spartans
author: Plutarch
content: “A Spartan woman, handing her son his shield, said: 'Either with it, or on it.'”

title: The Peloponnesian War
author: Thucydides
content: “The strong do what they can and the weak suffer what they must.”

title: Fragments
author: Archilochus
content: “The fox knows many things, but the hedgehog knows one big thing.”
